codeReview.summary: The Sync Beacon frontend wiring covers the main surfaces, but it does not actually show all remote machines as specified because machines sharing the same configured name are filtered out wholesale.

issues.description: `applyBeaconData` filters remote machines by comparing `machine.machineName` to the local machine name string. If two different machines intentionally share the same configured Sync Beacon machine name, every matching remote entry is hidden, violating the requirement to exclude only the local machine while displaying other machines' repo data. The frontend payload/merge logic has no stable machine identifier beyond the display name, so collisions make remote machines disappear from the panel.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Sync Beacon currently uses the configurable machine display name as the only machine identity in both frontend filtering and backend merging, so duplicate names collapse or hide distinct machines. This identity constraint is not documented in the mission AGENTS or library notes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: packages/ui/src/lib/sync-beacon-context.tsx:95 filters out any machine whose `machineName` equals the local configured name; apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands.rs:1101+ stores beacon records keyed by machine name, so no separate stable machine id exists.

summary: Reviewed handoff, transcript skeleton, and commit `1f89ed3`. The feature adds the requested settings/panel/timer wiring, but it has a blocking identity bug: remote machines are keyed and filtered only by display name, so two machines with the same configured name cannot both appear in the beacon panel. Report saved for scrutiny follow-up.
